dataset: Zotero library with seeded academic papers (Classic + ML papers)

pi_items.source: Task description specifies tagging papers with 'short-read' tag
Task description: 'Identify all papers in the library that are 5 pages or fewer in length'

pi_items.metadata_value: A Note on Two Problems in Connexion with Graphs
pi_items.verified_value: A Note on Two Problems in Connexion with Graphs
pi_items.source: Dijkstra, E.W. (1959), Numerische Mathematik 1: 269-271 (3 pages) - verified via Springer Nature and semantic scholar

pi_items.metadata_value: Molecular Structure of Nucleic Acids: A Structure for Deoxyribose Nucleic Acid
pi_items.verified_value: Molecular Structure of Nucleic Acids: A Structure for Deoxyribose Nucleic Acid
pi_items.source: Watson, J.D. & Crick, F.H.C. (1953), Nature 171: 737-738 (2 pages) - verified via Nature journal and PubMed

pi_items.metadata_value: A Method for the Construction of Minimum-Redundancy Codes
pi_items.verified_value: A Method for the Construction of Minimum-Redundancy Codes
pi_items.source: Huffman, D.A. (1952), Proceedings of the IRE 40(9): 1098-1101 (4 pages) - verified via IEEE Xplore and Google Scholar

pi_items.metadata_value: Mastering the Game of Go with Deep Neural Networks and Tree Search
pi_items.verified_value: Mastering the Game of Go with Deep Neural Networks and Tree Search
pi_items.source: Silver, D. et al. (2016), Nature 529: 484-489 (6 pages) - verified via Nature journal and Google Research

privileged_info_summary: All three ground truth papers are confirmed published works with verified page counts below the 5-page limit: Dijkstra's 1959 note on graph problems (3 pages, Numerische Mathematik), Watson and Crick's 1953 DNA structure paper (2 pages, Nature), and Huffman's 1952 coding theory paper (4 pages, Proceedings of the IRE). The distractor paper (AlphaGo, 2016) spans 6 pages in Nature, exceeding the threshold. All paper titles and publication details in the metadata match authoritative sources exactly.
